questions.question: Define the different types of security threats and provide examples of each.
keyPoints: Interception: unauthorized access to confidential information
Interruption: disruption of normal network or system functioning
Modification: alteration of data or system configurations
Fabrication: creation of fake data or messages

questions.question: Explain the OSI security model and its application in identifying and addressing security threats at each layer of the OSI protocol stack.
keyPoints: Description of the OSI security model
Explanation of security concerns at each layer (Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application)
Examples of countermeasures for each layer

questions.question: List and describe the components of a comprehensive network security model.
keyPoints: Network Segmentation: dividing a network into smaller, isolated segments
Firewalls: controlling incoming and outgoing network traffic based on security rules
IDPS Systems: monitoring network traffic for signs of unauthorized access or malicious activity
VPNs: encrypting and securing network traffic between two endpoints

questions.question: Compare and contrast the roles of firewalls, IDPS systems, and VPNs in preventing security threats and protecting network resources.
keyPoints: Description of the role of firewalls in controlling network traffic
Explanation of the role of IDPS systems in detecting and preventing intrusions
Discussion of the role of VPNs in encrypting and securing network traffic
Comparison of the strengths and weaknesses of each security measure

questions.question: Design a comprehensive network security plan that incorporates multiple security measures to protect against various security threats.
keyPoints: Description of the network security plan
Explanation of the security measures included (network segmentation, firewalls, IDPS systems, VPNs)
Discussion of the benefits and limitations of the plan
Examples of how the plan can be implemented and maintained
